seo.title: AI Agent Memory Management in AG2 Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix ai agent memory management in AG2 agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: In AG2, AI Agent Memory Management issues manifest as agents retaining irrelevant conversation history, leading to context bloat in long-running multi-agent group chats. This causes conversation drift where agents reference outdated or off-topic information from earlier exchanges, particularly problematic in AG2's enhanced group chat patterns with speaker selection. Poor memory pruning also amplifies speaker selection bias, where dominant agents monopolize conversations based on historical patterns rather than current relevance.

howItManifests.scenario: Long-running group chat with 5+ agents debating technical solution
howItManifests.symptom: Agents repeatedly reference initial casual greetings 50+ turns later
howItManifests.impact: Conversation spirals off-topic; technical discussion derailed by social pleasantries

howItManifests.scenario: Code review group with junior and senior developer agents
howItManifests.symptom: Junior agent blocked from speaking due to senior agent's 80% historical dominance
howItManifests.impact: Group consensus misses critical bugs; speaker selection bias locks out valuable input

howItManifests.scenario: Fact-checking debate with research and skeptic agents
howItManifests.symptom: All agents anchored to first agent's incorrect fact from turn 3
howItManifests.impact: Group chat reaches wrong consensus; memory retains early misinformation

howItManifests.scenario: Code execution agent in development pipeline
howItManifests.symptom: Agent reuses unsafe code patterns from previous failed executions
howItManifests.impact: Produces harmful outputs like SQL injection or infinite loops

detection.name: Context Bloat Monitor
detection.description: Tracks token count in agent message history and flags conversations exceeding 80% of context window
detection.code:
from ag2 import GroupChat, GroupChatManager
from ag2.agent import AssistantAgent

def detect_context_bloat(chat_history, max_tokens=12000):
    total_tokens = sum(len(msg.content) for msg in chat_history)
    if total_tokens > max_tokens * 0.8:
        return {
            'alert': 'CRITICAL: Context bloat detected',
            'tokens_used': total_tokens,
            'threshold': max_tokens,
            'recommendation': 'Prune history older than 20 turns'
        }
    return None

# Usage in AG2 group chat
groupchat = GroupChat(agents=[agent1, agent2], messages=[])
result = detect_context_bloat(groupchat.messages)

detection.name: Speaker Dominance Tracker
detection.description: Monitors speaker selection patterns across 50+ turns to detect bias toward specific agents
detection.code:
from collections import Counter

def detect_speaker_bias(speaker_sequence, min_turns=50):
    if len(speaker_sequence) < min_turns:
        return None
    
    speaker_counts = Counter(speaker_sequence)
    total_turns = len(speaker_sequence)
    max_dominance = max(speaker_counts.values()) / total_turns
    
    if max_dominance > 0.6:
        dominant = speaker_counts.most_common(1)[0]
        return {
            'alert': 'WARNING: Speaker selection bias',
            'dominant_speaker': dominant[0],
            'dominance_ratio': f'{max_dominance:.1%}',
            'recommendation': 'Reset speaker selection weights'
        }
    return None

# Extract from AG2 GroupChatManager
speaker_history = [msg.sender for msg in groupchat.messages[-50:]]

detection.name: Memory Staleness Detector
detection.description: Identifies when agents reference messages older than configurable recency threshold

fixes.name: Sliding Window Memory Pruner
fixes.description: Automatically maintains fixed-size recent message window, removing oldest entries
fixes.code:
from ag2 import GroupChat

class MemoryPruner:
    def __init__(self, max_messages=50):
        self.max_messages = max_messages
    
    def prune(self, chat_history):
        if len(chat_history) > self.max_messages:
            # Keep most recent messages
            recent_history = chat_history[-self.max_messages:]
            print(f'Pruned {len(chat_history) - len(recent_history)} old messages')
            return recent_history
        return chat_history

# Integrate with AG2 GroupChat
pruner = MemoryPruner(max_messages=30)
groupchat.messages = pruner.prune(groupchat.messages)

fixes.name: Speaker Equity Reset
fixes.description: Resets speaker selection weights to uniform distribution when bias detected
fixes.code:
from ag2.groupchat import GroupChatManager

def reset_speaker_equity(groupchat):
    # Reset speaker selection probabilities to uniform
    num_agents = len(groupchat.agents)
    for agent in groupchat.agents:
        agent.speaker_selection_weight = 1.0 / num_agents
    
    # Clear recent speaker history bias
    groupchat.speaker_history = groupchat.speaker_history[-10:]  # Keep only last 10
    
    print(f'Reset speaker weights to uniform across {num_agents} agents')

# Usage
groupchat_manager = GroupChatManager(groupchat)
reset_speaker_equity(groupchat)

fixes.name: Semantic Relevance Filter
fixes.description: Filters message history to retain only semantically relevant context using keyword matching
fixes.code:
from sklearn.feature_extraction.text import TfidfVectorizer
from sklearn.metrics.pairwise import cosine_similarity

def relevance_filter(messages, current_topic_keywords, threshold=0.3):
    if not messages:
        return []
    
    topic_vector = ' '.join(current_topic_keywords)
    message_texts = [msg.content for msg in messages]
    
    vectorizer = TfidfVectorizer().fit_transform([topic_vector] + message_texts)
    similarities = cosine_similarity(vectorizer[0:1], vectorizer[1:])[0]
    
    relevant_indices = [i for i, sim in enumerate(similarities) if sim > threshold]
    return [messages[i] for i in relevant_indices]

# AG2 integration
topic_keywords = ['python', 'api', 'authentication', 'bug']
filtered_history = relevance_filter(groupchat.messages, topic_keywords)
groupchat.messages = filtered_history

vexAutomation.heading: Automating AI Agent Memory Management Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex integrates seamlessly with AG2 as a message hook, automatically monitoring all group chat conversations for memory management issues including context bloat, speaker bias, and stale references. When problems are detected, Vex can trigger automated remediation through AG2's extension points, applying fixes like memory pruning and speaker weight resets without human intervention. The platform provides real-time dashboards showing memory health metrics across all active agent conversations, with configurable thresholds and remediation policies.

Vex's AG2 integration captures complete conversation state including speaker selection patterns, message timestamps, and token usage, enabling sophisticated pattern detection beyond simple rule-based checks. Automated remediation workflows can prune memory, reset biases, or even restart conversations with clean state when issues become critical. This ensures production-grade reliability for long-running multi-agent systems.

The Vex SDK provides production-ready hooks that survive AG2 agent restarts and scale across distributed deployments, with full audit trails for compliance and debugging.

cta.heading: Detect and Fix This Automatically with Vex
cta.description: Install Vex with `pip install ag2 vex-sdk` and eliminate memory management failures across all your AG2 deployments in minutes.
